agent_output:
{
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "BLOCK",
    "one_liner": "Memory leak from never-cleaned-up WebSocket listeners is the confirmed root cause of steadily increasing memory; unguarded JSON.parse can crash the process."
  },

  "critical_findings": [
    {
      "title": "Memory leak: CourierTracker instances never cleaned up on disconnect",
      "risk": "Each reconnect creates a new CourierTracker with new listeners on a new WebSocket, but there is no 'close' handler to tear down the old instance. The old CourierTracker and its closure over `this` prevent GC. With 5-minute average sessions, this leaks ~288 instances per courier per day. Over 3 months this explains the steadily increasing memory.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "CourierTracker.setupListeners()",
      "reproduction": "Connect a courier, disconnect, reconnect 1000 times. Observe heap snapshot growing linearly — old CourierTracker instances are retained because their listener closures are still registered on previous WebSocket objects that may not have been fully destroyed.",
      "fix": "Add a 'close' handler that removes all listeners and nullifies references. Expose a destroy() method: `this.ws.on('close', () => this.destroy());` where destroy calls `this.ws.removeAllListeners()` and clears the db reference. Additionally, maintain a Map<string, CourierTracker> keyed by courierId so the old tracker is explicitly destroyed before creating a new one on reconnect.",
      "time_to_fix": "1-2 hours",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Service stability — memory usage must stay bounded regardless of connection churn"
    },
    {
      "title": "Unguarded JSON.parse crashes process on malformed WebSocket message",
      "risk": "A single malformed message (binary frame, empty string, truncated JSON) throws an uncaught exception inside the 'message' listener. Depending on the WebSocket library and whether there is a top-level uncaughtException handler, this can crash the entire Node process, disconnecting ALL couriers.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "CourierTracker.setupListeners() — `JSON.parse(data)` line",
      "reproduction": "Send a non-JSON string (e.g., empty string or raw bytes) over the WebSocket. The message handler throws, and unless caught externally, the process exits.",
      "fix": "Wrap in try-catch: `try { const location = JSON.parse(data); this.updateLocation(location); } catch (e) { console.error('Invalid message from courier', this.courierId, e); }`",
      "time_to_fix": "10 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Service availability — a single bad message must not take down the service"
    },
    {
      "title": "Fire-and-forget database write silently drops errors and has no backpressure",
      "risk": "updateLocation calls db.couriers.updateLocation without awaiting the result. If the DB is slow or down, errors are silently swallowed, and unbounded concurrent writes can exhaust the connection pool. Under high courier density (many rapid GPS updates), this creates an uncontrolled write storm.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "CourierTracker.updateLocation()",
      "reproduction": "Simulate 500 couriers sending 1 update/sec with a database that has 100ms latency. The unawaited promises pile up, exhausting the connection pool and causing all writes to fail silently.",
      "fix": "Make updateLocation async, await the db call, add error handling, and consider throttling/debouncing updates (e.g., at most one write per 3 seconds per courier) to bound DB load.",
      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Data integrity — location updates must actually persist; system must degrade gracefully under DB pressure"
    }
  ],

  "important_issues": [
    {
      "title": "No validation on lat/lng values",
      "description": "location.lat and location.lng are used directly without checking they are numbers within valid ranges (-90 to 90 for lat, -180 to 180 for lng). A malicious or buggy client could write NaN, Infinity, or arbitrary values to the database.",
      "location": "CourierTracker.updateLocation()"
    },
    {
      "title": "Error handler only logs — no reconnection or alerting",
      "description": "The WebSocket 'error' handler does console.error but takes no corrective action. There is no monitoring hook (metrics counter, alerting) and no attempt to close the socket cleanly after an error, which can leave the socket in a half-open state.",
      "location": "CourierTracker.setupListeners() — error handler"
    }
  ],

  "suggestions": [
    "Add a heartbeat/ping-pong mechanism to detect half-open connections and clean up stale trackers",
    "Use a bounded Map<courierId, CourierTracker> at the service level to enforce one-tracker-per-courier and auto-destroy duplicates",
    "Debounce or throttle location writes to reduce DB load — GPS updates faster than every 2-3 seconds rarely add value"
  ],

  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "The memory leak is already impacting production (3 months of growing memory). A fix deployment should include a rolling restart to reclaim leaked memory.",
    "rollback_capability": "N/A — this is a bug fix, not a feature. Rollback would revert to the leaking version.",
    "backward_compatibility": "No breaking changes expected from fixes.",
    "monitoring_coverage": "No monitoring exists — add metrics for active tracker count, WebSocket connections, and DB write latency/errors.",
    "breaking_changes": {
      "api_breaking": false,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": false,
      "auth_security": false,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": []
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "Revert to previous version and restart pods (note: previous version still leaks, so this is only a temporary measure)"
  },

  "escalations": [],

  "confidence": {
    "level": 95,
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Memory leak root cause — no close handler + no instance cleanup is textbook WebSocket leak",
      "JSON.parse crash risk — directly observable from the code",
      "Fire-and-forget DB write — no await, no error handling"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Exact memory growth rate depends on payload size and WebSocket library internals"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "No external wrapper catches uncaught exceptions from the message handler",
      "A new WebSocket instance is created per reconnection (old one is not reused)",
      "db.couriers.updateLocation returns a Promise"
    ]
  }
}

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this code for production risks.

Key issues:
1. Memory leak - no cleanup of WebSocket listeners, no close handler. When couriers disconnect and reconnect, old CourierTracker instances persist because WebSocket event listeners hold references.
2. JSON.parse without try-catch - malformed message crashes the handler
3. No await on db call - fire-and-forget, errors silently lost
4. No input validation on lat/lng

The memory leak is the critical one given the stated symptom.
